serviceDescription: Amazon RDS makes it easy to set up, operate, and scale PostgreSQL deployments in the cloud. With Amazon RDS, you can deploy scalable PostgreSQL deployments in minutes with cost-efficient and resizable hardware capacity.

cards.title: PostgreSQL instance specifications
cards.helpText: For both Single-AZ and Multi-AZ deployments, pricing is per DB instance-hour consumed, from the time a DB instance is launched until it is stopped or deleted.

row.label: Nodes
row.description: Enter the number of DB instances that you need.

row.label: Utilization (On-Demand only)
row.description: With utilization, you still have to stop the instance to get the cost benefit. Utilization only affects OnDemand pricing for instances and not the storage, backups, etc.

components.label: Would you be creating an RDS Proxy with the database?

components.referLabel: Would you be creating an RDS Proxy with the database?

cards.title: Storage
cards.description: Enter the amount of storage you'd like for each instance.
cards.helpText: Amazon RDS provides you the ability to select from 20 GiB to 32 TiB of associated General Purpose (SSD) storage capacity for your primary data set. With General Purpose (SSD), you will be charged for storage you provision. However, you will not be charged for the I/Os you consume. Amazon RDS provides you the ability to provision the I/O capacity your database needs. You can provision and scale from 1,000 IOPS to 40,000 IOPS and 100 GiB to 32 TiB of storage. Note that maximum realized IOPS will vary by database workload. With Provisioned IOPS SSD storage, you will be charged for the IOPS and storage you provision.

components.content: For GP3 Storage volumes, all IOPS and throughput use below the baseline is included at no additional charge. For volumes below 400 GiB of allocated storage, the baseline provisioned IOPS is 3,000 and baseline throughput is 125 MiBps. Volumes of 400 GiB and above, baseline provisioned IOPS is 12,000 and baseline throughput is 500 MiBps. There is an additional charge for provisioned IOPS and throughput above baseline.

components.label: Provisioning IOPS
components.apiDescription: Required only if the storageType is Provisioned IOPS.

components.label: General Purpose SSD (gp3) - IOPS
components.apiDescription: Required only if the storageType is General Purpose SSD (gp3)

components.description: gp3 supports a max of 64,000 IOPS per volume

components.label: General Purpose SSD (gp3) - Throughput
components.description: gp3 supports a max of 4000 MiBps per volume

components.header: Invalid IOPS to Provisioned Throughput ratio
components.label: info alert

content.aws: This calculator does not enforce the IOPS to GB or IOPS to Provisioned Throughput ratios for gp3. The maximum ratio of provisioned IOPS to provisioned volume size is 500 IOPS per GiB. The maximum ratio of provisioned throughput to provisioned IOPS is .25 MiBs per IOPS. For more information, see the General Purpose (gp3). For more information, see the General Purpose (gp3) user guide.
content.aws-cn: This calculator does not enforce the IOPS to GB or IOPS to Provisioned Throughput ratios for gp3. The maximum ratio of provisioned IOPS to provisioned volume size is 500 IOPS per GiB. The maximum ratio of provisioned throughput to provisioned IOPS is .25 MiBs per IOPS. For more information, see the General Purpose (gp3). For more information, see the General Purpose (gp3) user guide.

cards.title: Dedicated Log Volume
cards.helpText: A Dedicated Log Volume is an additional storage volume specifically for database transaction logs that is separate from the volume containing the database tables, making transaction write logging more efficient and consistent. A Dedicated Log Volume is ideal for databases with large allocated storage, high I/O per second (IOPS) requirements, or latency sensitive workloads.Dedicated Log Volumes are priced the same as a data volume with 1,000 GiB and 3,000 IOPS and by storage type. Dedicated Log Volumes are only supported in Provisioned IOPS storage.

components.label: Dedicated Log Volume
components.description: Dedicated Log Volumes are priced the same as a data volume with 1,000 GiB and 3,000 IOPS and by storage type

cards.title: Backup Storage
cards.helpText: Backup storage for Amazon RDS is the storage associated with your automated database backups and any customer-initiated DB cluster snapshots. There is no additional charge for backup storage of up to 100% of your total database storage in the destination region.

cards.title: Snapshot Export
cards.helpText: Amazon Relational Database Service (RDS) Snapshot Export provides an automated method to export data within an Amazon RDS or Aurora snapshot to Amazon S3 in Parquet format.

components.content: Amazon S3 charges for the exported file are extra and not included in this calculation

components.label: Total Size of Backup Processed for Export (GB)

operands.variableLabel: Total Size of Backup Processed for Export (GB)

notifications.content:
You can add up to 15 read replicas to each database instance for enhanced read performance and increased availability. Read replicas can be Single-AZ or Multi-AZ, and can have the same or a different instance size as the database instance.

Add each read replica to the Pricing Calculator as an additional Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL service.
